text: A courier hands you a will naming {pair} heir to a chair, a debt, and half of {pair_other}. The two are {pair_kind}, which explains nothing and worsens everything.

outcomes.text: {pair} reads every clause twice. {pair_other} reads your face. The chair is apparently evidence.
effects: schedule 2 r13_heir_chair The inherited chair has been opened.

success.text: Your edits prevent a duel and accidentally grant you standing in the estate.
effects: schedule 3 r13_heir_chair The family requests its new legal expert.

fail.text: You move one comma and transfer the debt to yourself.
effects: schedule 2 r13_heir_chair The creditors found the chair.

text: The inherited chair contains a second will, three teeth, and a map drawn by someone who hated straight roads. {pair} and {pair_other} both claim the teeth.

outcomes.text: The route ends beneath a tax office floor. The family secret has municipal foundations.
effects: schedule 3 r13_heir_cellar The map has led below the tax office.

choices.text: Sell the chair and keep the map

outcomes.text: The chair sells immediately to a dentist with political ambitions.
effects: schedule 4 r13_heir_cellar Someone else reached the cellar first.

text: Below the tax office, {pair} finds ledgers proving the estate was built from money owed to {pair_other}'s family. The teeth belonged to the auditor.

choices.text: Return the estate to the injured line

outcomes.text: Ownership changes hands. So does the resentment, though it becomes more accurately addressed.
effects: schedule 4 r13_heir_hearing The inheritance hearing has been called.

choices.text: Destroy the ledger and split the estate

outcomes.text: The paper burns. The debt does not, but now it lacks page numbers.
effects: schedule 4 r13_heir_hearing The inheritance hearing lacks its best evidence.

text: At the hearing, {pair} speaks first. {pair_other} interrupts with the chair, now wearing a judge's wig. Nobody challenges its standing.

success.text: The court recognizes harm, kinship, and furniture as separate legal persons.
effects: schedule 5 r13_heir_feast The estate invites everyone to a reconciliation feast.

fail.text: The judge awards the estate to the chair. It immediately raises rent.
effects: schedule 5 r13_heir_feast The chair is hosting a victory feast.

choices.text: Ask both claimants what they actually want

outcomes.text: Neither wants the estate. Both want the other to admit the dead lied.
effects: schedule 3 r13_heir_feast The confession feast is ready.

text: The estate serves a final feast. {pair} and {pair_other} sit together beneath a portrait whose painted eyes keep changing sides.

choices.text: Toast the living instead of the estate

outcomes.text: The toast is short. The peace is not perfect, which may be why it lasts.

choices.text: Accept the chair as your fee

outcomes.text: The chair objects, then follows you anyway. It knows too much to remain furniture.

text: Bells ring beyond the regional border. A line of refugees arrives before the sound does, carrying sealed letters and one extremely healthy-looking goat.

choices.text: Open the gates and organize shelter

outcomes.text: Every roof gains a family. Every family gains an opinion about the roof.
effects: schedule 3 r13_refugee_goat The refugee goat has become politically important.

outcomes.text: The camp obeys. Hunger does not recognize the rope boundary.
effects: schedule 2 r13_refugee_goat The quarantined goat escaped.

text: The healthy goat has eaten the quarantine ledger and is now the only creature who knows the official headcount.

outcomes.text: Its first count is inaccurate but refreshingly free of favoritism.
effects: schedule 4 r13_refugee_work The new arrivals seek work.

success.text: You remember every name except the goat's. It files a complaint.
effects: schedule 3 r13_refugee_work The new arrivals seek work.

fail.text: You accidentally count the goat as six cobblers.
effects: schedule 3 r13_refugee_work Six imaginary cobblers need jobs.

text: The newcomers can repair roads, brew medicine, and argue in three tax dialects. Local guilds call this an invasion of competence.

outcomes.text: The first crew repairs a bridge and invents a shared insult for management.
effects: schedule 5 r13_refugee_fever A fever appears in the work camp.

outcomes.text: The permits expire at noon and become collectible by dusk.
effects: schedule 4 r13_refugee_fever A fever appears near the permit office.

text: A child in the camp develops a fever. The physician says it may be plague, weather, or an aggressive dislike of turnips.

outcomes.text: The fever breaks. So does the physician's certainty, which was overdue.
effects: schedule 3 r13_refugee_home The neighboring crisis has changed.

outcomes.text: Nobody leaves. By morning, everybody has exchanged soup and rumors.
effects: schedule 3 r13_refugee_home The sealed district demands an answer.

text: Word arrives that the bells have stopped. Some families want to return. Others have planted gardens and named the drainage ditch.

choices.text: Finance anyone who chooses to return

outcomes.text: Half go home. Half stay. Both groups begin writing each other about what the other is missing.

choices.text: Declare the camp a permanent quarter

outcomes.text: A neighborhood appears on the map before the cartographer approves it.

text: A merchant sells seeds that grow tiny locked strongboxes. The harvest is legal in this region and banned everywhere that has heard about it.

outcomes.text: By sunset, the crowd is watering the soil with investment contracts.
effects: schedule 3 r13_trade_harvest The first strongbox crop is ready.

outcomes.text: You own every seed except the one the merchant kept in their mouth.
effects: schedule 2 r13_trade_harvest Your private harvest has attracted inspectors.

text: The strongboxes ripen. Most contain coins. One contains soil, which may be how the plant reinvests.

choices.text: Share seed stock with neighboring regions

outcomes.text: Caravans depart carrying agriculture and future litigation.
effects: schedule 5 r13_trade_counterfeit Neighboring crops have produced counterfeit locks.

outcomes.text: You become briefly wealthy and permanently recognizable to seed inspectors.
effects: schedule 3 r13_trade_counterfeit Counterfeit locks flood the market.

text: Counterfeit strongboxes spread across the border. They open only for people who do not own them, creating a remarkably honest crime wave.

success.text: The new design works. Criminals complain about unfair innovation.
effects: schedule 4 r13_trade_embargo The old lock guild demands compensation.

fail.text: Your lock opens for anyone carrying soup.
effects: schedule 3 r13_trade_embargo The lock guild demands an embargo.

outcomes.text: You sell thieves boxes that rob them back. The economy becomes circular and furious.
effects: schedule 3 r13_trade_embargo The border closes around your inventory.

text: The lock guild declares an embargo. Every border gate closes except one operated by a clerk who accepts payment in interesting apologies.

outcomes.text: The patent is fourteen pages and recognizes the plant as co-inventor.
effects: schedule 5 r13_trade_fair The patent fair opens tomorrow.

choices.text: Smuggle seeds through the apology gate

success.text: Your apology is so interesting the clerk forgets to inspect three wagons.
effects: schedule 4 r13_trade_fair The illicit trade fair needs a keynote speaker.

fail.text: The clerk finds your apology derivative.
effects: schedule 4 r13_trade_fair The guild displays your confiscated seeds.

text: The first Interregional Strongbox Fair opens. Farmers, thieves, bankers, and one confused beekeeper share a ribbon.

outcomes.text: The boom survives because nobody owns all of it. This disappoints several excellent villains.

choices.text: Sell your stake at the opening bell

outcomes.text: You leave rich before the beekeeper asks what strongboxes do to honey.

text: A traveling saint declares {suspect}, the {suspect_trait} {suspect_prof}, innocent of {suspect_case}. Their halo is polished brass and squeaks in rain.

outcomes.text: The halo bears an archive inventory number filed after the alleged miracle.
effects: schedule 2 r13_saint_miracle The saint announces a public miracle.

choices.text: Join the procession to stay close

outcomes.text: You receive a sash, a hymn sheet, and access to the donation wagon.
effects: schedule 3 r13_saint_miracle The procession reaches the miracle square.

text: The saint heals a statue of {suspect} by removing a crack that was painted on this morning. The crowd gasps on schedule.

outcomes.text: The paint comes away on your thumb. Half the crowd cheers the trick anyway.
effects: schedule 3 r13_saint_donations The donation wagon leaves by the back road.

outcomes.text: Its axle marks lead toward the original archive.
effects: schedule 2 r13_saint_donations You catch the donation wagon after dark.

text: The wagon carries donations, forged witness statements, and a letter addressed to {suspect}. The seal is genuine. The handwriting is trying too hard.

outcomes.text: You seal it inside a cleaner envelope and sign every edge.
effects: schedule 3 r13_saint_witness The saint's former bookkeeper asks to meet.

choices.text: Use it to confront the saint now

outcomes.text: The saint calls it a sacred forgery, which is a confession wearing incense.
effects: schedule 2 r13_saint_witness A frightened bookkeeper follows you.

text: The bookkeeper says the saint was paid to protect {suspect}. Then they admit three suspects used the same payment name.

choices.text: Test the account against your evidence

success.text: Dates, ink, and payments align around one person.
effects: schedule 3 r13_saint_trial The public hearing begins.

fail.text: The numbers align around the bookkeeper's lunch expenses.
effects: schedule 3 r13_saint_trial The hearing begins with weak evidence.

choices.text: Protect the witness and trust the account

outcomes.text: Protection earns details fear had withheld.
effects: schedule 3 r13_saint_trial The protected witness is ready.

text: At the hearing, {suspect} waits beneath the squeaking halo. You have evidence, doubts, and one chance to put a name into history.

outcomes.text: You make the accusation and submit the full chain of evidence. The verdict will have to survive the facts.

choices.text: Refuse a verdict built on doubt

outcomes.text: The hearing adjourns. The saint flees, but an innocent name remains unbroken.

text: The guild has split over whether {pair} or {pair_other} invented the left-handed invoice. Their {pair_kind} has become everybody's paperwork.

outcomes.text: Both stories are convincing and mutually impossible.
effects: schedule 2 r13_guild_vote The guild calls an emergency vote.

choices.text: Back the person you know better

outcomes.text: Loyalty gains a chair and loses half the room.
effects: schedule 2 r13_guild_vote The factional vote is tied.

text: The vote is tied because {pair} counts apprentices while {pair_other} counts chairs. Two chairs abstain.

outcomes.text: Workers without titles enter the chamber and change the result.
effects: schedule 3 r13_guild_strike The losing faction calls a strike.

outcomes.text: Both sides pay. You vote for adjournment.
effects: schedule 2 r13_guild_strike Both factions strike against you.

text: The strike closes workshops across the region. {pair} brings demands. {pair_other} brings lunch, accidentally making the picket line stronger.

outcomes.text: By evening, insults become proposals with punctuation.
effects: schedule 4 r13_guild_saboteur Someone sabotages the negotiations.

outcomes.text: Business is excellent until both factions notice.
effects: schedule 3 r13_guild_saboteur Your workshop is blamed for sabotage.

text: Someone has smashed the guild's master stamp. {pair} accuses {pair_other}. The broken handle smells strongly of soup.

choices.text: Trace the soup before choosing sides

success.text: The trail leads to a chair that abstained from the vote.
effects: schedule 3 r13_guild_union The guild meets one final time.

fail.text: Every workshop serves the same soup. This is worse than no clue.
effects: schedule 3 r13_guild_union The final guild meeting begins angry.

choices.text: Replace the stamp and move on

outcomes.text: The new stamp reads APPROXIMATELY OFFICIAL.
effects: schedule 3 r13_guild_union The approximate guild charter is ready.

text: {pair} and {pair_other} present a joint charter. It bans hereditary chairs, recognizes apprentices, and requires soup labels.

outcomes.text: The guild reunites around rules broad enough to include its arguments.
They pay you in stamped invoices redeemable after the next schism.

text: A town walks past on hundreds of wooden legs. Its mayor leans from the clock tower and asks whether this road leads somewhere with lower property tax.

outcomes.text: The town follows, flattening only roads that needed widening.
effects: schedule 2 r13_walking_zoning The walking town requests zoning permission.

choices.text: Charge a road toll per house

outcomes.text: The mayor pays for every house except the jail, which claims diplomatic immunity.
effects: schedule 2 r13_walking_zoning The jail has filed a zoning appeal.

text: The town wants to stand here overnight. Local law defines a building as immobile, so every house has been charged with loitering.

choices.text: Register the town as a vehicle

outcomes.text: It receives one enormous license plate and a warning about turning signals.
effects: schedule 3 r13_walking_market The mobile market opens at dawn.

choices.text: Hide the town behind the hill

outcomes.text: Inspectors accept that the skyline has always had chimneys.
effects: schedule 2 r13_walking_market The hidden town opens its market.

text: The walking market sells local goods from places it crossed yesterday. Every souvenir is authentic to somewhere else.

outcomes.text: Roads fill with goods following the town's footprints.
effects: schedule 4 r13_walking_bridge The town reaches a narrow bridge.

outcomes.text: The book lists every shortcut and three towns that are avoiding this one.
effects: schedule 3 r13_walking_bridge The route ends at a narrow bridge.

text: The bridge can hold twelve wagons. The town weighs approximately nine hundred wagons and refuses to cross one bakery at a time.

success.text: Barrels become pontoons. The town floats badly but enthusiastically.
effects: schedule 3 r13_walking_choice The walking town reaches the far bank.

fail.text: The courthouse sinks first and continues hearing cases underwater.
effects: schedule 3 r13_walking_choice The damp town reaches the far bank.

choices.text: Reinforce the bridge with local timber

outcomes.text: The bridge survives. The forest sends an invoice.
effects: schedule 3 r13_walking_choice The town must choose where to settle.

text: The town reaches an empty valley and asks whether it should finally stop. Its oldest resident cannot remember a floor that stayed beneath breakfast.

outcomes.text: The legs fold beneath new streets. For the first time, the clock tower knows tomorrow's location.
The town turns toward the horizon. Years from now, maps will mark its migrations like weather.
